steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most effectively uses data from the graph. Only claims you can verify directly from the bar heights count. The passage is limited to organizations filing Form 990-PF.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — those that filed Form 4720 in 2005 because they engaged in self-dealing collectively paid a larger percentage of the total penalty assessed that year than those that filed Form 990 but also filed Form 4720 for the same reason.: Wrong. The graph covers only Form 990-PF filers; this choice goes out of scope. ✗
  • Choice B — those that filed Form 4720 because they had excess holdings in a business enterprise paid, on average, a larger penalty than those that filed Form 4720 because they engaged in self-dealing.: Wrong. The graph shows percentages, not averages you can calculate. ✗
  • Choice C — those that filed Form 4720 because they engaged in self-dealing paid an increasing percentage of the total penalty each year from 2003 to 2005.: Wrong. The bar heights don't support this claim. ✗
  • Choice D — those that filed Form 4720 in 2003 because they failed to meet the minimum charitable distribution requirement collectively paid a larger percentage of the total penalty assessed that year than those that filed Form 4720 in 2005 for the same reason.: Correct. The graph shows 55% in 2003 vs. 40% in 2005 for undistributed income — directly readable from the bars. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
